lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Sharing Opinions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Pricila Adamo | Aula 12 -- Sharing Opinions

listenings.text: If you ask me, the best part of traveling is the food. In my opinion, you really get to know a culture through its dishes. Some people prefer museums and old buildings, and that is fine, but I think the kitchen tells a deeper story. Take this city, for example. The markets are full of flavors you cannot find anywhere else. I would rather spend an afternoon at a food stall than in a fancy restaurant. To me, that is the real taste of a place.

For a long time, I was afraid to share my opinion in English. I worried that I would sound rude if I disagreed with someone. But I learned that you can disagree in a kind, polite way. Now, when I do not agree, I say something gentle first, like that is a good point, and then I add my own view. People respect an honest opinion, as long as you are warm about it. Sharing what you really think is how a simple chat becomes a real conversation.

extra_audio.text: First, the traveler shares his opinion that food is the best part of a trip. Then Pricila partly agrees but adds her own view about nature. Next, the traveler points out a good reason for visiting markets. After that, they realize they both enjoy local experiences. Finally, they agree to explore a food market together.
